idea.hook_en: Your civ forces you to swap identity every era — pick the new culture that preserves the most of your old bonuses or your empire collapses.
idea.hook_ru: Ваша цивилизация меняет облик каждую эпоху — выберите ту культуру, что сохранит больше прежних бонусов, или империя рухнет.
idea.mechanic: 3-era turn-based card pick. 6 stat bars (Faith/War/Gold/Science/Food/Culture). At each era end, 4 successor civ cards offer different stat carry-multipliers (×0.5..×1.5) plus a flat bonus. Pick one. After 3 eras the sum decides win/loss; any single bar below 20 after a transition collapses the run.
idea.pressure: 3 fixed era transitions + minimum stat floor (no timer)
idea.scope: single-session prototype, daily seed sets starting civ + successor pool

sources.summary: Discussion of Civilization VII's controversial mandatory civ-swap-per-era system — community split on whether it preserves enough continuity or feels jarring.

synthesizer_brief: Civ VII's swap mechanic is itself the design tension: the player loses continuity at era boundaries. Civ Pivot leans into that as the primary verb — every era you literally pick a new identity and compute how much of your old self survived. The cards expose carry multipliers visibly so the trade-off is legible, and the stat floor punishes greedy or careless transfers. Synthesis idea distilled to a 3-pick prototype to test the core 'legacy vs reinvention' tension that the Civ community was arguing about in the source post.
